title: EvaGeeks needs a mobile app for IOS and Android
display_title: EvaGeeks needs a mobile app for IOS and Android
wikitext:
[]: EvaGeeks needs a mobile app for IOS and Android             Quote Post by kameha » Sun Jan 03, 2021 9:40 pm I've been a longtime fan of Evangelion and recently wondered if Evageeks would ever consider making a Mobile app for IOS an Android? Could we get one?

[]: Re: EvaGeeks needs a mobile app for IOS and Android             Quote Post by Blockio » Mon Jan 04, 2021 8:38 am The problem with that is simply one of the effort necessary for it. Making a mobile app (not to mention maintaining one) requires time, effort and usually money for the infrastructure, all things we are ultimately lacking. The forum software itself is more than a few versions behind, just because we don't have the manpower of coders who can keep it up to date and make the many custom features work on the newer version, and so on and so forth. If we had the ressources to spare, it would absolutely be worth considering, but at the time, it's simply way out of scope

[]: Re: EvaGeeks needs a mobile app for IOS and Android             Quote Post by JoelcrNeto » Mon Jan 04, 2021 9:27 am There's an annual cost to maintain an iOS app in the App Store (I dunno if there is a free account), not to mention Apple's red tape to publish an app (based on my professional experience).

[]: Re: EvaGeeks needs a mobile app for IOS and Android             Quote Post by pwhodges » Mon Jan 04, 2021 2:48 pm There is an app called Tapatalk which some people find a good way to access forums on phones - it requires a plugin to be installed in the forum, which has not been done here. Tapatalk can be customised and skinned to become specific to a forum, but this has an associated charge. Since I first added Tapatalk to another forum I have become jaded by the changes and emails which are aimed at further monetising their app rather than enhancing its functionality - which in my view has degraded as a result of their changes. The improvement in forum skins which can adapt for mobile use has made such apps less attractive than they once were.
